source_title: \"They Created Bitcoin!\" Professor Jiang Exposes Why Every Technology Needs A \"Front Man\"│Jack Neel

interactions.summary: The host frames Jiang as a prediction-driven historian and asks future-looking questions; Jiang answers with a 2060 fork between AI-controlled obedience and human communities trying to preserve freedom, then gives 2045 as environmental systems collapse triggered by geomagnetic and solar disruption.
interactions.speaker_attribution: Interview exchange: SPEAKER_00 is host Jack Neel or ad read; SPEAKER_01 is Jiang unless noted.

interactions.summary: Jiang extends the environmental-collapse forecast, then answers the host's dominant-nation question by naming German and Japanese resilience, and distinguishes religious Greater Israel from transnational-capital Pax Judaica.
interactions.speaker_attribution: Interview exchange: SPEAKER_00 is host Jack Neel or ad read; SPEAKER_01 is Jiang unless noted.

interactions.summary: The host tests Jiang's Pax Judaica forecast; Jiang answers that elite actors play both sides, that Iran has surprised him by strategic resilience, and that Persia could become a counterweight to the Israeli-centered project.
interactions.speaker_attribution: Interview exchange: SPEAKER_00 is host Jack Neel or ad read; SPEAKER_01 is Jiang unless noted.

interactions.summary: Jiang argues population collapse and moral desensitization follow from borrowing against the future, then answers the Newton/2060 question with a new dark ages and an AI God fork; the host's ad interrupts before returning to whether today's God is AI.
interactions.speaker_attribution: Interview exchange: SPEAKER_00 is host Jack Neel or ad read; SPEAKER_01 is Jiang unless noted.

interactions.summary: Jiang answers the God/AI question by laying out Hermetic philosophy: mind, vibration, fractal correspondence, rhythm, dimensions, cause and effect, polarity, generation, love, eudaimonia, imagination, and Kantian moral spectatorship.
interactions.speaker_attribution: Interview exchange: SPEAKER_00 is host Jack Neel or ad read; SPEAKER_01 is Jiang unless noted.

interactions.summary: Asked which God-belief best controls society and why Jesus was killed, Jiang compares polytheism, monotheism, and no-God systems, then argues Jesus threatened the status quo by telling slaves and the oppressed they were divine souls.
interactions.speaker_attribution: Interview exchange: SPEAKER_00 is host Jack Neel or ad read; SPEAKER_01 is Jiang unless noted.

interactions.summary: Jiang answers the spy/terror/control and Satoshi questions by describing imperial sponsorship of Islamic extremism and then using game-theory questions to identify the American deep state or CIA as the likely blockchain beneficiary.
interactions.speaker_attribution: Interview exchange: SPEAKER_00 is host Jack Neel or ad read; SPEAKER_01 is Jiang unless noted.

interactions.summary: Jiang moves from Zuckerberg and the Winklevoss twins to front-person technology history: Bitcoin investment looks strange to him, while the 1970s credibility crisis made government-backed computing need cute public-facing nerds.
interactions.speaker_attribution: Interview exchange: SPEAKER_00 is host Jack Neel or ad read; SPEAKER_01 is Jiang unless noted.

interactions.summary: Jiang extends the front-person theory through HP, Stanford, Rockefeller/Carnegie, and charity, then answers science-as-religion by starting from COVID vaccine obedience.
interactions.speaker_attribution: Interview exchange: SPEAKER_00 is host Jack Neel or ad read; SPEAKER_01 is Jiang unless noted.

interactions.summary: The host pushes the religion-of-science idea; Jiang says COVID debate was suppressed, then claims COVID was a bioweapon or gain-of-function product tied to U.S. military subcontracting in China.
interactions.speaker_attribution: Interview exchange: SPEAKER_00 is host Jack Neel or ad read; SPEAKER_01 is Jiang unless noted.

interactions.summary: After joking about official U.S.-China enmity, the host asks the three biggest lies; Jiang names death as final reckoning and individual powerlessness as destructive lies.
interactions.speaker_attribution: Interview exchange: SPEAKER_00 is host Jack Neel or ad read; SPEAKER_01 is Jiang unless noted.

interactions.summary: Jiang rejects the idea that history is random by criticizing teleological progress stories, then explains elite longevity through attention and Trump as the convergence of global consciousness.
interactions.speaker_attribution: Interview exchange: SPEAKER_00 is host Jack Neel or ad read; SPEAKER_01 is Jiang unless noted.

interactions.summary: The host connects Trump to memes and bloodline power; Jiang calls Trump a psychic demon fed by emotion, then explains elite bloodline belief as a justification for rule by inherited divine superiority.
interactions.speaker_attribution: Interview exchange: SPEAKER_00 is host Jack Neel or ad read; SPEAKER_01 is Jiang unless noted.

interactions.summary: Jiang warns against DNA ancestry services in a bloodline conspiracy frame, then says secret societies often promote myths about themselves as recruitment and PR, not simply hidden power.
interactions.speaker_attribution: Interview exchange: SPEAKER_00 is host Jack Neel or ad read; SPEAKER_01 is Jiang unless noted.

interactions.summary: Asked what he is allowed to say and whether he is a spy, Jiang distinguishes abstract speculation from naming incriminating specifics, then explains his China freedom as a foreigner outside the Chinese citizen-control system.
interactions.speaker_attribution: Interview exchange: SPEAKER_00 is host Jack Neel or ad read; SPEAKER_01 is Jiang unless noted.

interactions.summary: The host asks how Jiang would read a mirror-image foreign influencer teaching Chinese topics; Jiang says he would be baffled, then narrates his own strangeness: Yale, China, rejecting money, and manifesting his unlikely public role.
interactions.speaker_attribution: Interview exchange: SPEAKER_00 is host Jack Neel or ad read; SPEAKER_01 is Jiang unless noted.

interactions.summary: Jiang explains why he made predictions publicly, why elite friends missed obvious events, and how Yale trauma taught him a false meritocracy/IQ/zero-sum/failure worldview he later had to overcome.
interactions.speaker_attribution: Interview exchange: SPEAKER_00 is host Jack Neel or ad read; SPEAKER_01 is Jiang unless noted.

interactions.summary: Jiang describes reinventing himself by dying to the Yale ego through cooking school, jiu-jitsu, stand-up, climbing, skydiving, and singing; humiliation becomes education in humility, empathy, resilience, ego, fear, and love.
interactions.speaker_attribution: Interview exchange: SPEAKER_00 is host Jack Neel or ad read; SPEAKER_01 is Jiang unless noted.

interactions.summary: Asked when he saw through illusion, Jiang describes meeting his wife, family replacing success, creative output, great-books teaching, Dante opening into love, and his videos as sparks rather than scripture.
interactions.speaker_attribution: Interview exchange: SPEAKER_00 is host Jack Neel or ad read; SPEAKER_01 is Jiang unless noted.

interactions.summary: The host asks for advice and one book; Jiang tells the Gay Talese story behind “don’t chase pussy,” then recommends Dante as divine truth and the Iliad as the transformative accessible book.
interactions.speaker_attribution: Interview exchange: SPEAKER_00 is host Jack Neel or ad read; SPEAKER_01 is Jiang unless noted.

interactions.summary: The closing exchange gives Jiang's public locations: YouTube Predictive History and PredictiveHistory.substack.com for current events in a structural lens.
interactions.speaker_attribution: Interview exchange: SPEAKER_00 is host Jack Neel or ad read; SPEAKER_01 is Jiang unless noted.

claims.claim: By 2060 Jiang predicts a civilizational fork: AI-controlled communities where people accept microchipped obedience versus weaker communities trying to preserve human freedom and imagination.
claims.temporal_scope: Forward-looking prediction made in the interview dated 2026-04-15 for roughly 2060.

claims.claim: By 2045 Jiang expects complete chaos resembling Bronze Age collapse, driven by geomagnetic excursion, solar flares, and the fragility of a fully digitized urban economy.
claims.temporal_scope: Forward-looking prediction made on 2026-04-15 for roughly 2045.

claims.claim: Jiang predicts a next-20-years perfect storm of environmental catastrophe that can shatter nation-states into resource-fighting city-states.
claims.temporal_scope: Prediction made on 2026-04-15 for roughly the 2040s.

claims.claim: Jiang distinguishes the Greater Israel Project as a religious Zionist territorial and messianic program, while Pax Judaica is transnational capital financing that project into an AI surveillance empire.
claims.temporal_scope: Definition and diagnosis in the 2026-04-15 interview.

claims.claim: Jiang says powerful billionaires and elite operators have no fixed loyalty; they position themselves amid chaos to maximize individual benefit.
Jiang revises his expectation for the Middle East war: he expected Iran to be devastated, but Iranian escalation control and narrative control make Persia a possible counterweight to Pax Judaica.
Jiang says modern middle-class abundance is an illusion financed by borrowing from the future, and war or supply-chain disruption will bring the bill due through famine and normalized brutality.
By 2060 Jiang predicts a new dark ages in which some elites try to create an AI God that makes people obedient, while others fight for freedom, imagination, and a new spiritual path.
Jiang defines God as the conscious movement of the universe: the universe is conscious, fractal, vibrating information, and human beings exist inside God while God is inside them.
Jiang says love opens the soul to collective consciousness, drives imagination, and makes creativity in the individual echo through the universe.
Jiang says polytheism encourages creative questioning, monotheism drives anxious energetic action and suffering, and no-God systems risk humans or emperors becoming godlike tyrants.
Jiang argues Jesus was likely crucified because his divine-soul message undermined Roman and Jewish authority by psychologically liberating slaves and the oppressed, creating economic damage.
Jiang claims Anglo-American empire encouraged Islamic extremism to sabotage the Soviet Union, counter Middle Eastern nationalism/socialism, and destabilize the region in ways that benefited Israel.
Jiang says game-theory analysis of Bitcoin asks who had the expertise, who benefited, and why secrecy mattered, leading him to the American deep state/CIA as the likely creator or sponsor.
Jiang treats the Winklevoss twins' early large Bitcoin investment after the Facebook settlement as anomalous evidence that Bitcoin had elite signaling or backing beyond a niche novelty.
claims.temporal_scope: Speculative evidentiary claim in the 2026-04-15 interview.

claims.claim: Jiang argues the post-Vietnam, post-Watergate U.S. government needed front people to make computers seem safe, cute, and innocuous despite Pentagon surveillance origins.

Jiang says the public billionaire-founder story is recent; earlier tycoons like Rockefeller and Carnegie functioned as agents of the City of London and used charity to disguise how much money they controlled.
Jiang argues COVID vaccine acceptance showed science functioning like religion because most people accepted authority rather than questioning logic, mutation, and evidence.
Jiang defines science-as-religion as truth claims without debate: during COVID, dissent was shut down and ostracized instead of explored.
Jiang claims COVID was a bioweapon or gain-of-function product supported by the U.S. military and subcontracted to China after Obama-era restrictions.
Jiang says the first great lie is that death is final; he treats death as beginning, rebirth, reset, and a mechanism of knowledge accumulation.
Jiang says the second great lie is that individuals are powerless; because humans reflect the universe, living well and being kind materially improve the world.
Jiang says modern people falsely believe history is teleological progress toward superiority, even though older or indigenous civilizations may have deeper meaning, balance, and purpose.
Jiang argues the secret to elite longevity is not the body but the mind: Trump may persist by occupying billions of people's attention until their convergent consciousness makes him godlike.
Jiang calls Trump a psychic demon fed by intense love and hate, suggesting attention and emotion empower him regardless of valence.
Jiang says sacred-bloodline belief helps elites explain why they should rule despite encountering smarter people: pure, divine, or heroic blood justifies inherited power.
Jiang speculates that DNA ancestry services could be used to find or suppress bloodlines, while marking the claim as conspiracy theory without evidence.
Jiang says secret societies promote myths about their own power because recruitment and political leverage depend on people believing the myth.
Jiang says abstract analysis of secret societies is tolerated, but naming powerful individuals with incriminating specifics or revealing confidential-source information would trigger retaliation.
claims.temporal_scope: Self-positioning and risk model stated on 2026-04-15.

claims.claim: Jiang says he is allowed to speak in China because he is a foreign English teacher outside the Chinese citizen bureaucracy and AI surveillance grid, as long as he avoids Chinese social media, public speeches, Chinese reporters, and local monetization.

Jiang says his career makes sense less as espionage than as a long pattern of strange choices: Yale to China, family bafflement, high-school teaching, rejecting study-abroad riches, and walking away from UN comfort.
Jiang says he manifested his public role by imagining the possibility, studying alternative media, and positioning himself for debate, conversation, and truth-seeking venues.
Jiang says he posted predictions publicly because he truly believed them and wanted a historical record that could prove or disprove him.
Jiang says Yale did not teach him power; as a poor alienated student, he dissociated and left with a naive meritocratic, IQ-centered, zero-sum, failure-terrified worldview.
Jiang says he redesigned his Yale education by entering ordinary humiliating practices where elite credentials meant nothing, using failure to dismantle ego.
Jiang defines real education as risk-taking, trying new things, self-reflection, humility, empathy, and resilience, not elite schools and grades.
Jiang says marriage and family let him see success as illusion; letting go of success made him more creative and moved him into great-books teaching.
Jiang says Dante taught him that love is the secret of the universe: a conscious universe speaks back if one opens with faith and love.
Jiang says his lectures are not scripture but sparks meant to inspire people to seek truth for themselves.
Jiang presents Gay Talese's advice “don't chase pussy” as a lesson drawn from talent, literary ambition, sexual research, marital damage, and the power of love.
Jiang says Dante's Divine Comedy is the greatest book and truth embedded in poetry, while Homer's Iliad is the one broadly accessible book that transforms students.
Jiang says he is now easy to find publicly and names Predictive History on YouTube plus his Substack for geopolitics and current events in a larger structural lens.

signature_moments.moment: The 2060 future splits between people happy to be microchips and minorities salvaging what makes humanity human.
signature_moments.source_phrase: perfectly happy being a microchip
signature_moments.why_it_matters: This gives the interview its central freedom-versus-obedience image.

signature_moments.moment: A solar flare in New York becomes Jiang's concrete image of digital civilization collapsing into cold, thirst, and sanitation failure.
signature_moments.source_phrase: the car won't work, the power plants will give out
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It translates abstract geomagnetic risk into everyday urban breakdown.

signature_moments.moment: The nation-state gives way to city-states fighting over water.
signature_moments.source_phrase: the nation state then gives way to the city state
signature_moments.why_it_matters: This is the institutional consequence of the catastrophe model.

signature_moments.moment: Jiang says never bet against the Japanese after rehearsing Mongol, Meiji, and postwar resilience.
signature_moments.source_phrase: never, ever bet against the Japanese
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It condenses his civilizational resilience model.

signature_moments.moment: Iran's resilience surprises Jiang and forces him not to discount Persia.
signature_moments.source_phrase: we should not discount Persia
signature_moments.why_it_matters: This is a live update to his prior expectation of Iranian devastation.

signature_moments.moment: Persia and Russia become possible Gog and Magog in the eschatological script.
signature_moments.source_phrase: Gog and Magog represent Persia and Russia
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It links geopolitical trade blocs to religious war imagination.

signature_moments.moment: Modern abundance is a debt against the future whose bill comes due in war.
signature_moments.source_phrase: borrowed from the future and we've mortgaged the future
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It gives the collapse argument its moral economy.

signature_moments.moment: AI God is presented as a false end of history built on obedience.
signature_moments.source_phrase: once this AI God is in place
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It sharpens the anti-surveillance spiritual argument.

signature_moments.moment: God is not outside the universe; God is the universe moving consciously.
signature_moments.source_phrase: we are inside God, but God is also inside us
signature_moments.why_it_matters: This is the packet's metaphysical center.

signature_moments.moment: Jiang turns morality into acting before the gaze of all past and future beings.
signature_moments.source_phrase: the entire world the entire universe both past and future it's all watching
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It makes ethics a cosmic spectatorship test.

signature_moments.moment: One God creates skyscrapers and high-speed rail but forgets to ask why.
signature_moments.source_phrase: no one ever asked the question why are we doing this
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It captures his critique of monotheistic energy without purpose.

signature_moments.moment: A slave who knows he is a divine soul becomes an economic threat.
signature_moments.source_phrase: you're a divine soul, you shouldn't be a slave
signature_moments.why_it_matters: This turns theology into material rebellion.

signature_moments.moment: Blockchain becomes a Trojan horse for surveillance and black-budget finance.
signature_moments.source_phrase: almost like a Trojan horse
signature_moments.why_it_matters: This is the memorable turn in his Satoshi theory.

signature_moments.moment: The value of Bitcoin depends on people believing it is beyond authority.
signature_moments.source_phrase: only if people believe that this was transparent, open, and beyond authority
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It explains why secrecy is structurally necessary in the theory.

signature_moments.moment: The computer had to be open-sourced to nerds because government had no credibility.
signature_moments.source_phrase: let's just open source this to nerds
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It condenses the front-man thesis for personal computing.

signature_moments.moment: HP refusing Wozniak's computer becomes suspicious because the employer normally owns the IP.
signature_moments.source_phrase: No, go sell yourself. It's yours.
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It supplies the anomaly in his Apple origin story.

signature_moments.moment: The self-made billionaire becomes an agent wearing the American dream as disguise.
signature_moments.source_phrase: agents, representatives of the city of London
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It reverses the heroic founder myth.

signature_moments.moment: A mutating virus turns the vaccine into a fortification the virus can learn to evade.
signature_moments.source_phrase: you're training the virus to be much more evasive
signature_moments.why_it_matters: This is the compact logic of his vaccine critique.

signature_moments.moment: Science becomes religion when debate disappears.
signature_moments.source_phrase: How much debate happened during the COVID epidemic? None.
signature_moments.why_it_matters: This is his cleanest definition of the science-faith inversion.

signature_moments.moment: The alleged U.S.-China bioweapon link becomes proof the world does not work like official enemy stories.
signature_moments.source_phrase: the world probably doesn't work the way you think it does
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It ties conspiracy diagnosis to geopolitical theater.

signature_moments.moment: Death becomes a reset that lets the soul learn rather than a final reckoning.
signature_moments.source_phrase: death allows for rebirth and rejuvenation
signature_moments.why_it_matters: This underwrites his anti-fear ethic.

signature_moments.moment: Powerlessness is recast as a lie that absolves people of agency and responsibility.
signature_moments.source_phrase: absolved themselves of both agency as well as responsibility
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It makes kindness a cosmological action rather than private virtue.

signature_moments.moment: Modern people are empty vessels with bank accounts while indigenous communities have a story.
signature_moments.source_phrase: empty vessels trying to increase the number in our bank account
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It turns progress into spiritual poverty.

signature_moments.moment: Trump becomes the convergence point of individual realities.
signature_moments.source_phrase: Donald Trump becomes the convergence of all individual realities on planet Earth
signature_moments.why_it_matters: This is the bridge between media attention and godhood.

signature_moments.moment: Trump is a psychic demon who treats hate and love as the same food.
signature_moments.source_phrase: For him, hate, love is the same thing.
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It is the sharpest formulation of the attention model.

signature_moments.moment: Bloodline ideology lets mediocre elites call themselves reincarnated divine beings.
signature_moments.source_phrase: my blood is better. My blood is pure.
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It explains hierarchy as mythic self-protection.

signature_moments.moment: The Illuminati becomes more PR campaign than omnipotent machine.
signature_moments.source_phrase: more of a PR campaign than it's actually a powerful force
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It complicates conspiracy by making belief itself the power source.

signature_moments.moment: If enough people believe a secret-society myth, it becomes true.
signature_moments.source_phrase: Consciousness is reality itself
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It ties the political myth back to the metaphysics of attention.

signature_moments.moment: China's surveillance grid is aimed at collective networks, not random foreign teachers outside the system.
signature_moments.source_phrase: you're not interested in individual behavior. You're interested in collective behavior.
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It clarifies his theory of Chinese control.

signature_moments.moment: He chooses freedom by refusing lucrative Chinese fame.
signature_moments.source_phrase: I choose freedom, but I also choose not to engage in lucrative opportunities in China
signature_moments.why_it_matters: This is the personal tradeoff behind his public role.

signature_moments.moment: The United Nations is described as royal comfort and meaningless existence.
signature_moments.source_phrase: I was treated, like royalty... And I walked away.
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It shows his repeated refusal of conventional success.

signature_moments.moment: The YouTube strategy begins as making stupid predictions that either humiliate him forever or get him on Tucker Carlson.
signature_moments.source_phrase: make some stupid predictions and hope they turn out correct
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It explains the channel as deliberate risk-taking, not accidental virality.

signature_moments.moment: Obvious reality could not pass through the skulls of Yale and Harvard friends.
signature_moments.source_phrase: impossible to relay obvious reality to them
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It explains why he turned private arguments into public prediction.

signature_moments.moment: Yale alienation made him miss power rather than decode it.
signature_moments.source_phrase: I wasn't actually observing how power works
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It reverses the host's assumption about elite education as insight.

signature_moments.moment: The chef refusing to touch his dishes punctures Yale superiority.
signature_moments.source_phrase: I'm not eating this crap. Go away, man.
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It is the comic humiliation that starts the education reversal.

signature_moments.moment: Ego and fear become the two greatest enemies.
signature_moments.source_phrase: your two greatest enemies are ego and fear
signature_moments.why_it_matters: This distills the whole autobiographical transformation.

signature_moments.moment: He and his wife are two sparks navigating chaos to find each other.
signature_moments.source_phrase: two sparks drifting in the universe
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It gives the metaphysics an intimate human image.

signature_moments.moment: Dante speaks one word: love.
signature_moments.source_phrase: Love is the great secret of the universe
signature_moments.why_it_matters: This is the source's spiritual center of gravity.

signature_moments.moment: The lectures are not the truth; they are ignition.
signature_moments.source_phrase: ignited a spark in a lot of people
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It sets the public read's relation to Jiang's persona.

signature_moments.moment: The best advice is bluntly anti-distraction and comes from a writer who knew the cost of chasing desire.
signature_moments.source_phrase: Don't chase pussy.
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It is crude, memorable, and tied to the love-versus-appetite theme.

signature_moments.moment: Dante is truth itself embedded in poetry.
signature_moments.source_phrase: truth itself embedded in the poetry
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It preserves the force of Jiang's Great Books hierarchy.

signature_moments.moment: Jiang describes himself as everywhere now.
signature_moments.source_phrase: I happen to be everywhere now
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It closes the interview by showing the scale of the public persona he earlier described manifesting.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: Jiang uses it as the analogy for total systems collapse after environmental and technological breakdown.
A future social order where people accept surveillance, microchips, and obedience as normal.
Religious Zionist project to achieve the Promised Land from Egypt to Iraq and beyond, with Jerusalem and the Third Temple as eschatological center.
Transnational-capital project to use Greater Israel as a base for finance, mercenaries, imported labor, energy, data centers, and AI surveillance.
The sequence of conflict intensification Jiang says Iran is controlling strategically.
An eschatological war script Jiang connects to some readings of Persia and Russia against Israel.
A future artificial authority mistaken for God and used to make people obedient, rational, and controlled.
Jiang's term for the chaotic 2060 world of catastrophe, war, and no central authority.
The framework Jiang uses for mind, vibration, correspondence, polarity, generation, and divine consciousness.
Greek flourishing; Jiang uses it as the daily test of creativity, learning, growth, and love.
A sayings collection Jiang treats as possibly early and aligned with a Gnostic divine-spark Jesus.
Worldview in which death is release and humans are divine sparks seeking knowledge beyond material slavery.
Jiang's method of asking capability, beneficiary, and secrecy questions to infer hidden actors.
A technology Jiang says looks open while enabling monitoring and financing.
Public founders Jiang says make state-backed or institutional technologies appear personal, cute, and harmless.
The post-Vietnam, Church Committee, Watergate distrust that required indirect promotion of computers.
The idea that scientific authority can become unquestionable truth rather than debate and exploration.
Jiang's term for tycoons he says represented deeper financial power rather than self-made wealth.
Research Jiang says made the virus mutate unusually fast and was subcontracted because of U.S. restrictions.
A possible vaccine side effect Jiang uses as an example of insufficient questioning after mass rollout.
Jiang's claim that dying enables reset, learning, and renewed growth.
The capacity Jiang says every person has to improve the universe by living well.
The belief Jiang criticizes that history moves toward progress and modern superiority.
The idea that a person persists by implanting himself in collective consciousness.
Jiang's image for an attention entity strengthened by intense emotions.
A conspiracy framework Jiang says elites use to imagine inherited sacred rule.
A secret-society myth Jiang describes as recruitment media and political leverage.
Used as shorthand for reality shaped by perception and belief.
The data system Jiang says profiles Chinese citizens and their social networks through ID and payment infrastructure.
The tolerated position Jiang says foreign teachers occupy in China.
For Jiang, imagining a possible future and maneuvering himself into position for it.
Rogan, Dore, the Weinsteins, Tucker, and similar spaces Jiang saw as truth-seeking conversation venues.
Jiang's former belief that the best ideas and best work would naturally win without friends, money, or connections.

glossary_terms.term: Three Things Yale Didn't Teach Me
glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: Jiang's article naming IQ worship, zero-sum competition, and fear of failure as educational/psychological problems.
Jiang's phrase for replacing elite instruction with ordinary practices that force humility.
The two inner enemies Jiang says block love and openness.
The Dante-derived insight that love opens the universe and guides one toward light.
The effect Jiang hopes his lectures have: awakening independent truth-seeking.
Dante's poem; Jiang calls it a word from God and the greatest book ever written.
Homer's epic; Jiang recommends it as accessible, fun, and permanently transformative.
Jiang's YouTube channel and public identity for videos.
His stated frame for writing about current events and geopolitics on Substack.

chronology_notes.note: On 2026-04-15 Jiang gives dated 2045 and 2060 future predictions rather than a timeless model.
